parentQuestion: Histology would be best defined as a study of ___.

scaffolds.explain: Yes! Biology = study of life. Cardiology = study of heart. Pattern!
Correct! From Greek 'histos' meaning tissue or web.

scaffolds.q: HISTOLOGY = histo (tissue) + logy (study) = study of tissues.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Put the word parts together to get the meaning.

scaffolds.q: CYTOLOGY is the study of CELLS (cyto = cell).
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Different term for different level of organization.

scaffolds.q: Histology uses microscopes to examine tissue structure.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Tissues are too small to study in detail with the naked eye.

scaffolds.q: Gross anatomy studies structures visible WITHOUT a microscope.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Gross anatomy = large scale. Histology = microscopic scale.

scaffolds.q: Histology examines how cells are organized into tissues.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Looking at tissue slices under the microscope to see cell arrangement.

scaffolds.q: Histology is the study of TISSUES, not cells or gross structures.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Cytology = cells. Histology = tissues. Gross anatomy = organs.

scaffolds.q: The answer is TISSUES - histology is the study of tissues.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Not cells (cytology), not cell chemistry (biochemistry), not gross structures.
